items.situation: two students talking about a fern one is caring for while a sister is studying abroad

conversation.speaker: Emma
conversation.text: Hey Tom, did you remember to water the fern this morning?

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: Oh shoot. I was rushing to get to economics, and I completely forgot.

conversation.speaker: Emma
conversation.text: It's been three days now. The leaves were starting to droop yesterday.

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: That's not great. I promised your sister I'd keep it alive while she was studying abroad.

conversation.speaker: Emma
conversation.text: Well, fortunately ferns are pretty tough. Just soak it now and put it back in the indirect light.

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: Should I move it back to the front window? It seemed to be doing better there last week.

conversation.speaker: Emma
conversation.text: Actually no — the afternoon sun was scorching the new fronds. The hallway spot is better.

conversation.speaker: Tom
conversation.text: Okay, hallway it is. And I'll set a reminder on my phone for every other morning.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: How to keep a houseplant alive while caring for it
options.B: Whether to move a houseplant to a sunnier location
options.C: The challenges of houseplant care during exam periods
options.D: Why ferns are particularly difficult to grow indoors

questions.explanation: 对话从'你浇水了吗'开始，围绕如何让蕨类植物存活展开（位置、浇水频率、提醒），主旨即植物养护。B、C只触及部分细节；D与对话不符——Emma说蕨类'pretty tough'。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Tom?

options.A: He has clear experience taking care of many houseplants
options.B: He plans to return the fern to his sister very soon
options.C: He had been forgetting his responsibility to water the fern
options.D: He prefers the hallway spot for purely aesthetic reasons

questions.explanation: Tom承认完全忘记，且Emma指出已三天未浇水，可推断他一直在忽略浇水责任。A与他状态相反；B对话无相关信息；D Emma说放在走廊是因为前窗下午阳光过强，是功能而非美学。

items.situation: two students figuring out a meeting time for a group presentation

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Are you free Tuesday afternoon to work on the group presentation?

conversation.speaker: Mark
conversation.text: I've got the chemistry lab from 2 to 4, but I'm open after that.

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: How about 5 PM in the library study room?

conversation.speaker: Mark
conversation.text: Five works, but I have to grab a quick dinner first. Could we push it to 5:30?

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Sure, 5:30 is fine. I'll book the room on the second floor.

conversation.speaker: Mark
conversation.text: Great. Want me to bring the slide deck so we can work off the same version?

conversation.speaker: Sarah
conversation.text: Yes, please. I'll send Mia the time so she knows when to join us.

questions.stem: What time did the speakers finally agree to meet?
questions.explanation: Mark请求推迟到5:30，Sarah回复'Sure, 5:30 is fine'，最终时间确定为5:30 PM。A是Mark的化学实验时间；C是初次提议的时间；D无依据。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Mia?

options.A: She originally organized the group project from the beginning
options.B: She refused to meet on Tuesday for personal reasons
options.C: She typically works at the campus library on weekdays
options.D: She is part of the same project group

questions.explanation: Sarah说'send Mia the time so she knows when to join us'，明显表明Mia也是该小组成员需要加入此次会议。A、B、C均无依据。

items.situation: two friends recommending a history podcast to each other

conversation.speaker: Lily
conversation.text: You've been listening to that history podcast nonstop. What's it called again?

conversation.speaker: Ben
conversation.text: 'Forgotten Empires.' Each episode covers a civilization most people barely remember.

conversation.speaker: Lily
conversation.text: Like which ones?

conversation.speaker: Ben
conversation.text: Last week was the Garamantes in the Sahara. They built underground water tunnels through solid rock to grow crops two thousand years ago.

conversation.speaker: Lily
conversation.text: Wait, in the desert?

conversation.speaker: Ben
conversation.text: Exactly. And the host explains how new satellite imagery is changing what archaeologists think about them.

conversation.speaker: Lily
conversation.text: Okay, that sounds way better than another true-crime series. How long are episodes?

conversation.speaker: Ben
conversation.text: About forty-five minutes. New ones drop every Wednesday.

questions.stem: What is the focus of the podcast Ben recommends?

options.A: Modern archaeological techniques used in deserts
options.B: The construction of ancient water systems worldwide
options.C: Civilizations that most people barely remember
options.D: The history of true-crime documentary podcasts

questions.explanation: Ben明确说每期讲'a civilization most people barely remember'，这是节目核心。A、B只是单期具体细节；D是Lily比较时提到的不同体裁，不是这个节目内容。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Lily?

options.A: She has grown tired of true-crime podcast series
options.B: She plans to download the podcast immediately after the conversation
options.C: She has already heard of the Garamantes from a course
options.D: She prefers visual content like documentaries over podcasts

questions.explanation: Lily说'way better than another true-crime series'，'another'与不耐烦语气表明她对真实犯罪节目已感厌倦。B无依据；C她'in the desert?'的反应显示她不熟悉Garamantes；D与对话场景不符。

items.situation: two roommates working out a fair chore and quiet-hours arrangement

conversation.speaker: Maya
conversation.text: Hey, can we talk about the apartment for a minute?

conversation.speaker: Jake
conversation.text: Sure. What's up?

conversation.speaker: Maya
conversation.text: I've been doing the dishes after every meal we share, and it's getting old. Can we work out a fair split?

conversation.speaker: Jake
conversation.text: Honestly, I didn't notice. I thought we were rotating. Let's actually write something down this time.

conversation.speaker: Maya
conversation.text: Yeah. And while we're at it, can we agree on quiet hours? I have early labs three mornings a week.

conversation.speaker: Jake
conversation.text: How about no music or speakerphone after eleven?

conversation.speaker: Maya
conversation.text: Eleven works. And maybe we vacuum the living room every other Saturday — alternating who does it.

conversation.speaker: Jake
conversation.text: Done. I'll write it all up on the whiteboard tonight so we both see it.

questions.stem: What are the roommates mainly trying to do?

options.A: Decide whether to renew their apartment lease together
options.B: Plan a cleaning party with mutual friends from school
options.C: Discuss buying new kitchen appliances for the apartment
options.D: Set up a fair schedule for shared household tasks

questions.explanation: 对话从洗碗轮值开始，扩展到安静时间和打扫客厅，主旨是共同制定家务和共享空间的公平安排。A、B、C都未在对话中涉及。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about Maya?

options.A: She rarely uses the apartment's shared living room
options.B: She had been bothered by the situation for some time
options.C: She prefers to handle all the cleaning herself
options.D: She plans to move out at the end of the semester

questions.explanation: Maya说'it's getting old'，表明她已经为此事困扰一段时间才开口。A无依据；C与她要求公平分担矛盾；D无相关信息。

items.situation: two friends debating whether a new cafe is worth the higher prices

conversation.speaker: Zoe
conversation.text: Did you try that new espresso place on Maple Street yet?

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: Yeah, last Thursday. Six dollars for a cortado seemed steep, honestly.

conversation.speaker: Zoe
conversation.text: That's almost twice what the campus coffee bar charges.

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: I know. But the espresso shot itself was actually outstanding — way better than what I get on campus.

conversation.speaker: Zoe
conversation.text: So you think it's worth the price?

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: Once a week, maybe. Not every morning. The bagels they sell are also really good, but they're seven dollars each.

conversation.speaker: Zoe
conversation.text: Seven? For a bagel?

conversation.speaker: Alex
conversation.text: With cream cheese, sure, but still. I think the campus bagels are perfectly fine.

conversation.speaker: Zoe
conversation.text: Got it. So I should go for the espresso but skip the bagel.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?

options.A: Whether the new cafe is worth its higher prices
options.B: How to find cheaper coffee options near the campus
options.C: Why specialty coffee has become more expensive recently
options.D: The differences between cortados and other espresso drinks

questions.explanation: 对话围绕新咖啡馆6美元cortado和7美元百吉饼的性价比展开评估，Zoe最终结论体现了核心问题。B、C、D均与对话核心无关。

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the man's view of the cafe's bagels?

options.A: He thinks they are slightly better than the campus bagels
options.B: He plans to order them despite the high price
options.C: He considers them overpriced for what they offer
options.D: He prefers the cafe's bagels to its espresso

questions.explanation: Alex说7美元百吉饼后停顿表示并不值，并补充'campus bagels are perfectly fine'，可推断他认为定价过高。A与他说法相反；B与他的态度相反；D对话无依据。
